blocks.text:
Done — I created a persistent memory capturing the long-running opencode session.

Quick summary
- Memory ID: 069bf6cb-71c4-7545-8000-5d42b0d04b0e
- What I saved: session_id ses_4326bf609ffeXGhu5dUJboC2aL, project path, observed start/end (2026-01-17 → 2026-01-20T07:42:09Z), approx duration (~3 days), likely contents (many edits/tests/merges), and recommended next steps.
- Provenance (events attached to the memory): 069b8965-bfda-7eb3-8000-83f1e0687670, 069bf6c5-6bed-706f-8000-5f83a869e9c8, 069bf6c6-1beb-7a38-8000-a0d803d8c360, 069bf6c7-b24d-79d7-8000-c49af32cd364, 069bf6c8-bf46-7317-8000-c6cdf367a5b2.

Recommended next steps (I can do any of these for you)
1) Enumerate every event for the session and attach notable event IDs (file edits, test runs, merges, design notes) to the memory. Example SQL I’ll run against replay.db:
   SELECT id,timestamp,event_type,title,content FROM events WHERE session_id='ses_4326bf609ffeXGhu5dUJboC2aL' ORDER BY timestamp;
   Path used previously: /Users/saxenauts/Documents/personal/syke/experiments/runs/clean_minimal/replay.db
2) Generate an aggregated activity report for the project (last 30 days) — counts by day, longest sessions, active contributors, and recent gaps (good for onboarding/retrospective).
3) Add an automated memory rule suggestion: persist sessions when duration > 2 hours OR event_count > 200. (We can tune thresholds after collecting a week or two of data.)

Next action — what would you like me to do now?
- Option A: Run the SQL to list all events for ses_4326bf609ffeXGhu5dUJboC2aL and return a table + notable event IDs (I can then attach them to the memory).
- Option B: Generate the 30-day aggregated activity report for the project.
- Option C: Configure the automated memory rule suggestion (I’ll only persist it as a draft recommendation unless you ask me to enable it).
- Option D: Nothing else for now — just keep the saved memory.

If you want Option A, I’ll run the SQL and return the results (and a short provenance
